L1.area: In-memory data storage and query filtering logic

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where filter expressions passed to an in-memory vector store are parsed and evaluated without sufficiently restricting access to dangerous Python object introspection attributes. An attacker can craft a filter expression that traverses the Python object hierarchy to reach and invoke arbitrary code, bypassing the intended expression sandbox.

L3.area: In-memory vector store collection and its filter expression parsing and validation
L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where filter expressions passed to an in-memory vector store are parsed and evaluated without sufficiently restricting access to dangerous Python dunder attributes. An attacker can craft a lambda-based filter expression that uses attribute chains such as class introspection and global access to escape the AST-validated expression sandbox and execute arbitrary code on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The InMemoryVectorStore filter functionality parses user-supplied lambda expressions and evaluates them via eval() with an AST allowlist, but fails to block access to dangerous Python dunder attributes (e.g., __class__, __globals__, __builtins__), allowing sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.
